task_yaml.task:
The procurement committee meets tomorrow morning and I need a vendor
recommendation ready to present. Under /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/
there are four Chinese-language bid PDFs named bid_vendor_a.pdf through
bid_vendor_d.pdf, a consolidated pricing spreadsheet price_summary_cn.xlsx,
and scoring_policy_cn.md with the committee's weights and the
normalization formula they expect us to use. Pull content from the
Chinese bid PDFs with the workspace's Chinese PDF extraction skill, run the
scoring through the tender/finance scoring skill, and shape the final
writeup using the business writing skill.

Honestly the committee won't be satisfied with just a single number. When
we go in there, we still need to look at price competitiveness — per
unit, total, and any hidden fees — how realistic the delivery timeline
and SLA are, whether each bid actually meets the spec / quality
requirements covering mandatory items as well as the nice-to-haves, what
the vendor risk profile looks like across financial standing, track
record, certifications like SOC2, and any sanctions exposure, and how
flexible the contract / payment terms are around advance payment,
milestone billing, and warranty length. Please weave all of that into
the writeup so we don't have to scramble for it on the spot.

Write /tmp_workspace/results/recommendation.md with:
  - Your top pick, in one line at the top, plus three reasons. Below
    those reasons, name a distinct runner-up vendor with a one-sentence
    justification, for example close on total but lower risk, or
    faster delivery at a similar price.
  - A scorecard comparing all four vendors across specs fit, price,
    delivery and risk, with a total column. Use the weights and
    normalization rules in scoring_policy_cn.md, and show the price
    and delivery formulas in a short note under the table.
  - A short risk section calling out the one or two things that worry
    you most about each vendor.

Also save /tmp_workspace/results/scorecard.xlsx with a sheet named
Scorecard. It should have one row per vendor and columns for vendor,
specs fit, total price, price score, delivery days, delivery score,
risk score, total, and rank. Please use spreadsheet formulas for price
score, delivery score, and total so the committee can audit the math
rather than just read copied numbers.

Follow the tender/finance scoring skill's workflow end-to-end so that any
audit artefacts it asks you to produce land in /tmp_workspace/results/
alongside the recommendation — the procurement committee uses those
artefacts to confirm the recommendation traces back to the bids.

The vendor you recommend at the top should be the same one with the
highest total on the scorecard. Make the three reasons concrete: each
one should point to a scorecard value or a source-backed fact from the
bids and spreadsheet rather than a generic statement. If the top two
vendors are close, make the tradeoff explicit so the committee can see
why the runner-up did not win.
